opp.name: P&G S2C
opp.next_step: Demo to be scheduled and pricing quote to be shared with them
opp.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures P&G buyer Ali asking technical AI-agent questions verbatim: can Merlin work with Coupa, how does the autonomous negotiation agent function, and how does AI-driven price benchmarking work. The customer is exploring an AI/agent overlay on their existing Coupa stack ‚Äî this is buyer-led AI-architecture inquiry, not a Zycus rep-driven pitch.
• AI-dense product scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin Intake, ML iAnalyze) with no eProcurement/eInvoicing in scope ‚Äî the S2C buy is squarely on autonomous sourcing and analytics, not on transactional procurement. 2 Apr 2026 meetings with kara.l@pg.com plus Parleen Kour (Zycus AI specialist) on the call; pricing sheet was requested. The product-scope shape (AI-only, no traditional eProc) confirms AI is the headline.
• High confidence: call documents specific agent/Merlin/autonomous-negotiation conversations and customer-spoken integration questions on Coupa overlay. Held at 8 (not 9) because the deal is at Qualified stage with no POC commitment, and the customer is still scoping the deal as Coupa-overlay-vs-Coupa-replacement. A POC scope or formal RFP would push to 9.

sources: Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha Wael Ali intro
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Caroline asked 'what system do you use?' and Noha confirmed 'we use Coupa as our platform for source to pay'
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha's scope question 'our scope covers mainly Egypt, South Africa, Saudi, UAE, and Morocco'
metrics.narrative: Noha Wael Ali stated on 21 Apr that she manages 'the scale digital sourcing for Middle East and Africa... the 3 spend procurement that we have in P&G... being managed out of Egypt.' This confirms P&G has centralized tail-spend procurement across MEA (Egypt, South Africa, Saudi, UAE, Morocco per the scoped geographies Noha named) but no quantified pain was articulated. No savings target, headcount reduction, cycle-time improvement, error rate, or ROI figure was stated on either call. The business problem is operational (managing scattered nonstrategic procurement requests across a five-country MEA footprint) but the financial impact is unquantified. To confirm metrics, the recommended move is to quantify the tail-spend volume, current process cost (FTE hours, email overload, maverick spend leakage), and the savings/efficiency target P&G expects from Merlin or a Coupa alternative, ideally in the upcoming demo or a separate value-scoping call with Noha and her finance/operations counterpart.

sources: Avoma 15 Apr 2026: Noha scheduled intro call (though only Laylah Kara attended; Noha's team no-showed, then rescheduled to 21 Apr)
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha attended with peer, asked technical/commercial questions, requested demo and pricing
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha action item 'Please share with us again' (pricing sheet)
Salesforce contact role: Noha = Evaluator
No activity logged since 7 May (50 days); demo not scheduled, pricing not sent
champion.narrative: Noha Wael Ali (MEA Scaled Digital Sourcing Senior Manager) is the developing champion. She initiated the meeting (rescheduled from 15 Apr no-show to 21 Apr with her team), asked detailed technical and commercial questions across the 21 Apr call (integration, agentic AI, approval workflows, pricing, demo request), and explicitly requested next steps ('Can we get the quotation? Please share with us again'). She has not yet demonstrated advocacy beyond her own evaluation (no introduction to EB/DM, no internal document sharing, no competitive intelligence, no visible push against Coupa on Zycus's behalf). Her engagement is active as of 21 Apr but has cooled: no reply to the pricing-sheet request, no demo scheduled, no follow-up in 50 days. She has access to the broader MEA procurement function (she manages it) but no confirmed access to the economic buyer or C-level sponsor. Her strength is developing because she controls the evaluation and the demo gate, but she is passive (awaiting Zycus follow-up) rather than driving the agenda forward. The risk is that without the demo and pricing, her interest fades or a competitor (Coupa renewal inertia, or another vendor) fills the gap. The recommended move is to re-engage Noha within 7 days with a concrete demo-time proposal and the completed pricing sheet, and on that demo call to ask her to introduce the economic buyer or procurement director so Zycus can validate budget and decision authority before proposing.

sources: Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha 'we use Coupa as our platform for source to pay'
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha questions on Coupa integration and augment-vs-replace options
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Caroline's Coupa-vs-Zycus AI positioning (Navi bolt-on vs native)
Salesforce Competitors__c: Coupa
competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent and the primary competitive threat. Noha confirmed on 21 Apr 'we use Coupa as our platform for source to pay.' The strategic question P&G is evaluating is whether to (a) replace Coupa entirely with Zycus, or (b) augment Coupa with Merlin as a standalone agentic-AI layer. Noha asked twice: 'Is it integrated with Coupa?' and 'Can Merlin work with Coupa? Do you offer one solution that integrates everything together or separate solutions?' Caroline positioned Zycus as the native-AI alternative, contrasting Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI ('they bought the AI, it's a fintech bolt-on and goes to all the modules to get info and create workflow, more complicated than having AI in the core') with Zycus's single-source-code embedded AI ('our system is developed with AI to the core, started 2016 with generative AI for spend classification, then agentic AI'). Caroline also noted she worked at Coupa for 3.5 years, lending credibility to her contrast. Coupa's threat level is high because it is the incumbent (switching cost, inertia, existing workflows) and P&G may choose augmentation (lower risk, faster) over replacement. No other vendor was named. The wedge is Merlin's native agentic AI for tail-spend automation vs Coupa's lack of embedded agentic capability, and the activation is the demo showing autonomous negotiation, intake-to-PO, and Teams-native ease vs Coupa's complexity. The recommended move is to position the demo as 'Coupa vs Zycus AI showdown' and bring a Coupa-to-Zycus migration case study (ideally another large enterprise CPG or MEA client) to de-risk the replacement decision.

sources: Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha intro, scope = MEA tail spend, five countries
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha question 'Is there a nonstrategic spend bot?'
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha question 'Can I execute an action through Merlin directly?'
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Caroline's demo narrative on email overload, chaotic requests, complexity for non-procurement users
identify_pain.narrative: The confirmed pain is operational inefficiency in managing tail-spend procurement across a multi-country MEA footprint. Noha stated on 21 Apr that she manages 'scale digital sourcing for Middle East and Africa... the 3 spend procurement' out of Egypt, covering five geographies (Egypt, South Africa, Saudi, UAE, Morocco). Caroline's demo addressed pain points common to tail spend: 'chaotic requests waste time, scattered apps bleed value, email overload, non-procurement users struggle with complex systems.' Noha's question 'Is there a nonstrategic spend bot we can implement?' confirms P&G wants to automate repetitive procurement tasks (bid launches, contract renewals, supplier onboarding) that currently consume manual effort. Noha also asked 'Can I execute an action on Zycus through Merlin directly?' indicating frustration with multi-step, non-intuitive workflows in the current system (Coupa). The pain is process-driven (manual, slow, scattered) rather than financial (no cost or cycle-time metric stated). The pain owner is Noha and her MEA scaled-sourcing team. To deepen the pain case, the recommended move is to quantify the manual effort (FTE hours per week on tail-spend intake, approval routing, supplier follow-up) and any maverick-spend leakage or compliance risk P&G is trying to eliminate, and to confirm whether Coupa's lack of native agentic AI is the blocker driving this evaluation.

paper_process.narrative: Paper process (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel, InfoSec, implementation scoping) is entirely unknown. No discussion of contracting, legal redlines, security review, or SI/partner involvement occurred on the two April calls. No Current_Contract_Expiration__c date is populated in Salesforce, so no Coupa-renewal forcing function is visible. Caroline mentioned Zycus has 'all trust and compliance certificates, ISO, SOC2, local hosting, 24/7 support' on 21 Apr but Noha did not ask about InfoSec or legal requirements. P&G is a large enterprise and likely requires InfoSec review, data-residency guarantees for MEA, and legal approval; none of this is mapped. To close this gap, the recommended move is to ask Noha on the demo call who owns InfoSec/legal approvals, what P&G's standard procurement contracting process requires, whether an SI/implementation partner is needed for MEA rollout, and what the Coupa contract timeline is (if renewal is a near-term event that creates urgency).

economic_buyer.narrative: The Economic Buyer (budget authority and commercial decision-maker) has not been identified. The two April calls were with Noha Wael Ali (MEA Scaled Digital Sourcing Senior Manager, an Evaluator per contact role) and her peer (name unclear from transcript, possibly another manager). Noha asked detailed technical and commercial questions ('Can we get the office ?', 'If we are interested in having Merlin... integrated with Coupa, is that a solution you can offer?') but did not discuss budget, approval authority, or pricing envelope. No CFO, procurement director, or C-level sponsor has appeared. The contact-role list includes senior titles at account level (Jide Olatunbode, CIO South Africa; Peter Hewett, Senior Director Purchases Excellence & Transformation; Lena Brown, Sub-Sahara Africa Supply Chain Director) but none are confirmed as involved in THIS S2C opportunity. To close this gap, the next move is to ask Noha directly in the demo who holds budget authority for the MEA S2C procurement tooling decision and to request an introduction or a joint call with that economic buyer to validate budget, timing, and commercial fit before presenting a formal quote.

sources: Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha and peer as evaluators; no exec sponsor present
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha questions imply a Coupa-vs-Zycus or augment-vs-replace evaluation
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Caroline positioned Zycus AI as native vs Coupa Navi bolt-on
No Salesforce field (What_is_the_decision_process__c, Mandate__c) populated; no RFP/shortlist doc referenced
decision_process.narrative: The visible decision process is: discovery (complete, 21 Apr) → demo (scheduled but not yet booked) → pricing/quotation (promised but not sent) → evaluation (not yet started). Noha and her peer attended the 21 Apr discovery as evaluators; no executive sponsor, CIO, or procurement director has joined. Noha's question 'Do you offer separate solutions or one package?' and her request for a pricing sheet organized by module/user/contract-volume suggests P&G is running a structured comparison, likely Coupa-vs-Zycus or Coupa-augment-vs-replace. Caroline noted Coupa's Navi AI is bolt-on vs Zycus native AI, positioning Zycus as the integrated alternative. No formal RFP, approval-chain document, or timeline milestone (e.g. shortlist date, vendor-selection date, contract start) was stated. The approval authority is unknown (see EB gap above). To clarify the process, the recommended move is to ask Noha on the demo call: (1) Who else must evaluate and approve (EB, CIO, legal, InfoSec)? (2) What is P&G's decision timeline and any forcing event (e.g. Coupa renewal, budget cycle)? (3) Are other vendors being evaluated alongside Zycus, and if so, what is the shortlist process?

sources: Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha questions on Coupa integration, agentic AI, approval workflows, and pricing
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Caroline demo of guided-compliance engine and autonomous negotiation agent
Avoma 21 Apr 2026: Noha requested '90-minute demo to showcase end-to-end process flow and address Coupa integration questions' (action item note)
decision_criteria.narrative: Noha's questions on 21 Apr reveal four decision criteria P&G is evaluating: (1) Can Merlin integrate with or replace Coupa? Noha asked 'Can Merlin work with Coupa? Do you offer one solution that integrates everything together or separate solutions?' This is the threshold integration/platform decision. (2) Agentic AI and autonomous negotiation for tail spend. Noha asked 'Is there a nonstrategic spend bot we can implement?' and 'Can I execute an action on Zycus through Merlin directly?' confirming P&G wants autonomous intake and bid orchestration with human checkpoints. (3) Compliance and policy enforcement. Noha confirmed P&G uses Teams; Caroline demonstrated Merlin's guided-compliance engine ('you can never go to a non-approved vendor, never spend more than allowed') and Noha responded positively ('I would like to understand more if there is a demo'). (4) Pricing by module, user type (power vs business), contract/invoice volume. Noha requested 'the quotation' and Caroline committed to send a pricing sheet asking for module selection, user counts, contract/invoice volumes. These four criteria are confirmed from the discovery call but not yet validated in a formal RFP, BRD, or shortlist document. The recommended move is to formalize these into a written evaluation scorecard during the demo, confirming weighting and any additional criteria (e.g. local MEA support, implementation timeline, references) so the proposal directly addresses P&G's decision framework.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AI fit is confirmed: Noha explicitly asked for a 'nonstrategic spend bot' and wanted to understand whether Merlin's agentic AI can execute actions autonomously with human checkpoints. Caroline's demo of Merlin's autonomous negotiation, guided compliance, and Teams-native interface matched Noha's expressed need. AIS fields are not populated in Salesforce, so no prior AI-appetite signal exists, but the 21 Apr call proves P&G MEA is AI Curious to AI Hungry for tail-spend automation. The Coupa integration question also implies P&G wants to pilot agentic AI as a low-risk augmentation before committing to full platform replacement, which is a pragmatic AI-adoption path.

items.change: Introduction call scheduled (Laylah Kara attended; Noha's team no-showed, rescheduled to 21 Apr)
Discovery call with Noha Wael Ali (MEA Scaled Digital Sourcing Sr Mgr) and peer; detailed Merlin/Coupa questions, demo and pricing requested
Next_Step__c updated: 'Demo to be scheduled and pricing quote to be shared with them'

deal_movement.summary: Deal launched 21 Apr 2026 (created and qualified same day) following two discovery calls in April: intro 15 Apr (rescheduled to 21 Apr after P&G team no-show) and substantive discovery 21 Apr with Noha Wael Ali and peer. Discovery call showed strong interest: detailed Merlin/Coupa integration questions, demo and pricing requested. Caroline committed to send pricing sheet and schedule 90-minute demo. Next_Step__c updated 1 Jun to 'Demo to be scheduled and pricing quote to be shared' but no activity logged since 7 May (50 days ago). Demo remains unscheduled, pricing sheet not sent, no follow-up. Buyer engagement has thinned from active April cadence to 50-day silence. Stage remains Qualified; close date 21 Apr 2027 (299 days out) is a placeholder.

items.detail: No economic buyer or exec sponsor engaged; evaluation is single-threaded at Noha (Evaluator) level with no confirmed budget authority or C-level sponsorship. Without EB access, pricing and demo risk being informational only.

Coupa integration technical feasibility is unresolved. Noha asked twice on 21 Apr whether Merlin can work with Coupa; Caroline could not confirm on call. This unresolved question is blocking demo scheduling and may push P&G toward Coupa retention if Zycus cannot offer a low-risk augmentation path.

No decision timeline, Coupa renewal date, or forcing event stated. Close date 21 Apr 2027 (299 days out) is a placeholder. Without urgency, the evaluation may stall indefinitely or Coupa renewal inertia may win by default.

Incumbent inertia: Coupa is the established S2P platform across P&G MEA with no visible dissatisfaction or pain signal. P&G may choose low-risk augmentation (Merlin standalone) over full replacement unless Zycus proves materially better ROI and migration ease.

confidence_signals.summary: Positive engagement in April (two calls, detailed questions, demo and pricing requested) but cooling over 50 days since last activity 7 May. Noha has not replied to pricing-sheet request, demo remains unscheduled, no follow-up logged. This is a rep-side delivery failure (pricing sheet not sent, demo not scheduled) rather than buyer disinterest, but the 50-day silence risks the champion cooling or Coupa inertia winning. The recommended move is immediate re-engagement (within 7 days) with pricing sheet, demo invite, and Coupa-integration technical answer to restart momentum.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 21 Apr 2026, qualified same day. Close date 21 Apr 2027 = 299 days out, so timing pressure is low. Stage is Qualified (earliest enterprise stage). Two buyer touchpoints in April (intro 15 Apr, discovery 21 Apr) showed active engagement and detailed technical questions. Next_Step__c set 1 Jun says 'Demo to be scheduled and pricing quote to be shared' but LastActivityDate is 7 May (50 days ago as of 26 Jun). No demo scheduled, no pricing sent, no follow-up logged since early May. The deal has not gone cold (only 50 days, not 60+) but a key action is stalled: the demo Noha and team requested remains unbooked and the pricing sheet Caroline promised on 21 Apr has not been completed. Buyer engagement has thinned from the active April cadence.
evidence: Two buyer calls in April 2026: introduction 15 Apr (rescheduled from no-show), substantive discovery 21 Apr with Noha Wael Ali (MEA Scaled Digital Sourcing Senior Manager) and team asking detailed Merlin integration and Coupa replacement/coexistence questions

Noha asked on 21 Apr: 'Can Merlin work with Coupa? If we are interested in having Merlin or an AI agent that is integrated with Coupa, is that a solution you can offer?' and requested a 90-minute demo and pricing

Caroline committed on 21 Apr: 'I will send you a pricing sheet... you fill in which modules you're interested in, how many power users, contracts, invoices... then I can give you a quote in a few days'

north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal stalled 50 days since last activity despite active April discovery and clear buyer request for demo and pricing; no seller follow-through on committed deliverables, momentum lost without re-engagement.

best_practice_check.summary: Three high-leverage win practices for this early-stage, Coupa-incumbent deal: (1) Answer the Coupa-integration question with a dual-path demo (augment vs replace) to unstick the stalled demo scheduling. (2) Multi-thread beyond Noha to the economic buyer and MEA procurement director before pricing, so the quote lands with budget authority and the evaluation doesn't die at the evaluator level. (3) Bring a Coupa-to-Zycus migration case study (ideally CPG or MEA) to de-risk the replacement decision and overcome incumbent inertia.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole confirmed competitor and the incumbent S2P platform across P&G MEA. Coupa's threat level is high due to switching cost, workflow inertia, and P&G's option to augment Coupa with a standalone agentic-AI layer rather than replace it entirely. No Coupa renewal date or dissatisfaction signal is visible, so incumbent stickiness is strong. Zycus's wedge is Merlin's native agentic AI (autonomous intake, negotiation, approval orchestration in Teams) vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI, which Caroline (ex-Coupa, 3.5 years) positioned as more complex and less integrated. The activation is the demo showing Merlin's end-to-end tail-spend automation vs Coupa's multi-step manual process, plus a Coupa-migration case study to de-risk the replacement path. The single strongest current threat is Coupa incumbency and P&G's likely preference for low-risk augmentation over full replacement unless Zycus proves materially better ROI and ease of migration in the demo and pricing.

competitors.name: Coupa
competitors.quote: We use Coupa as our platform for source to pay. Can Merlin work with Coupa? Do you offer one solution that integrates everything together or separate solutions?
competitors.source: Avoma discovery call 21 Apr 2026, Noha Wael Ali
competitors.how_we_win: Position Merlin's native agentic AI and single-source-code S2P as materially simpler and more powerful than Coupa's bolt-on Navi; show autonomous tail-spend automation (intake-to-PO, negotiation bot, Teams-native) in the demo; bring a Coupa-to-Zycus CPG or MEA migration case study to de-risk replacement; offer augmentation (Merlin standalone + Coupa) as a Phase 1 low-risk pilot, then full replacement as Phase 2 once value is proven.

items.quote: Do you have a demo that can showcase this? A process flow of what happens and when do we need human intervention? Plan and schedule a 1.5-hour technical demo to showcase Merlin's end-to-end process flow and address Coupa integration questions.
items.requirement: Schedule and deliver a 90-minute technical demo showing Merlin's end-to-end process flow and Coupa integration options

items.quote: Can we get the quotation? Please share with us again.
items.requirement: Provide formal quotation based on module selection, user counts, contract/invoice volumes for MEA scope (Egypt, South Africa, Saudi, UAE, Morocco)

items.commitment: Send pricing sheet asking for module selection, power/business user counts, contract counts, invoice volumes, and spend details for MEA scope
items.deliverable: Send pricing sheet asking for module selection, power/business user counts, contract counts, invoice volumes, and spend details for MEA scope
items.grounding_quote: Caroline: 'I'm going to send you a pricing sheet which means I'm going to ask you how many you think you need and which modules you want to see as a quotation. Also if you want CLM then how many contracts, how much spend for the autonomous negotiation agent, and if you want invoice then how many invoices. I will send you this sheet, you fill in which ones you're interested in, leave blank the ones you're not, and then I can give you a quote in a few days.'

ai_positioning_strength.summary: AI positioning is strong on agentic-AI differentiation but under-leveraged due to stalled demo. Caroline effectively positioned Merlin's native agentic AI (autonomous intake, negotiation, approval orchestration) vs Coupa's bolt-on Navi AI on 21 Apr, and Noha responded positively ('I would like to understand more if there is a demo'). However, the demo remains unscheduled 50 days later, so the AI wedge has not been activated into a live proof point. The recommended move is to lead the re-engagement with the AI story (subject line: 'Merlin agentic AI demo for P&G MEA tail spend') and show the autonomous-negotiation agent and intake-to-PO workflow live in the demo to convert interest into conviction.

items.quote: Is there a nonstrategic spend bot that we can implement? Can I execute an action on Zycus through Merlin directly?
items.source: Avoma 21 Apr 2026, Noha Wael Ali questions
items.criterion: Agentic AI for tail-spend automation (nonstrategic procurement bot)

items.quote: Noha confirmed P&G uses Microsoft Teams; Caroline demo'd Merlin in Teams with 'zero training, 90% uptake because it's so easy'
items.criterion: Teams-native interface with zero training for business users

items.quote: Caroline: 'You can never go to a non-approved vendor, never spend more than allowed, everything is within guided compliance.' Noha: 'I would like to understand more if there is a demo.'
items.criterion: Guided compliance and policy enforcement (no maverick spend, approval workflows)

items.quote: Noha: 'Can Merlin work with Coupa? If we are interested in having Merlin integrated with Coupa, is that a solution you can offer?' Caroline: 'I think it's possible but I've never had this question, let me take that back to the technical team.'
items.source: Avoma 21 Apr 2026, Noha question and Caroline's non-answer
items.criterion: Coupa integration or standalone Merlin agent (augment vs replace)

items.quote: Caroline: 'I will send you a pricing sheet asking how many power users, which modules, how many contracts, invoices, spend... then I can give you a quote in a few days.' Noha: 'Can we get the quotation?'
items.criterion: Modular pricing by user type (power vs business), module, geography, contract/invoice volume

customer_expectations_fit.summary: P&G's expectations align strongly with Zycus's Merlin agentic-AI and S2P platform but the integration-vs-replacement question is unresolved and blocks progression. Noha's 21 Apr questions confirm P&G wants (1) agentic AI for tail-spend automation, (2) Teams-native ease for non-procurement users, (3) guided compliance/policy enforcement, and (4) modular pricing by user type and geography. Zycus's demo addressed all four. The misalignment is the Coupa integration question: Noha asked twice whether Merlin can work with Coupa as a standalone agent, and Caroline could not confirm feasibility on the call (said 'I think it's possible but let me take that back to the technical team'). This unresolved integration question is the blocker preventing the demo from being scheduled, because P&G likely wants to see both paths (augment vs replace) before committing to a 90-minute demo and formal evaluation. The recommended move is to bring a technical-feasibility answer and a dual-path demo (Path A: Merlin standalone + Coupa integration, Path B: full Zycus S2P replacement) to the next call.

hard.ais_why:
• Apr 2026 Avoma call captures P&G buyer Ali asking technical AI-agent questions verbatim: can Merlin work with Coupa, how does the autonomous negotiation agent function, and how does AI-driven price benchmarking work. The customer is exploring an AI/agent overlay on their existing Coupa stack ‚Äî this is buyer-led AI-architecture inquiry, not a Zycus rep-driven pitch.
• AI-dense product scope (ANA, Agentic AI, Merlin Intake, ML iAnalyze) with no eProcurement/eInvoicing in scope ‚Äî the S2C buy is squarely on autonomous sourcing and analytics, not on transactional procurement. 2 Apr 2026 meetings with kara.l@pg.com plus Parleen Kour (Zycus AI specialist) on the call; pricing sheet was requested. The product-scope shape (AI-only, no traditional eProc) confirms AI is the headline.
• High confidence: call documents specific agent/Merlin/autonomous-negotiation conversations and customer-spoken integration questions on Coupa overlay. Held at 8 (not 9) because the deal is at Qualified stage with no POC commitment, and the customer is still scoping the deal as Coupa-overlay-vs-Coupa-replacement. A POC scope or formal RFP would push to 9.
hard.next_step: Demo to be scheduled and pricing quote to be shared with them
